devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच – । दृष्ट्वाऽत्मनि जये व्यग्रान् नृपान् हसति भूरियम् । अहो मा विजिगीषन्ति मृत्योः क्रीडनका नृपाः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca – dṛṣṭvā aho mā vijigīṣanti mṛtyoḥ krīḍanakā nṛpāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Perceiving how kings are eagerly occupied with the plans of conquering her, this earth laughs at them saying, “How stupid! These kings who themselves are but playthings of (the god of) Death aspire to conquer me!

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां द्वादशस्कन्धे तृतीयोऽध्यायः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ dvādaśaskandhe tṛtīyo
translation: Though they are aware (of the inevitability of death) these kings have reposed over-confidence in (the longevity of) their personal body which is momentary like a bubble of water. Hence the ambition or desire of these rulers of men is definitely futile.

devanagari: पूर्वं निर्जित्य षड्वर्गं जेष्यामो राजमन्त्रिणः । ततः सचिवपौराप्तकरीन्द्रानस्य कण्टकान् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: pūrvaṃ nirjitya ṣaḍvargaṃ jeṣyāmo rājamantriṇaḥ tataḥ sacivapaurāptakarīndrānasya kaṇṭakān
translation: (They plan in their minds):

devanagari: एवं क्रमेण जेष्यामः पृथ्वीं सागरमेखलाम् । इत्याशाबद्धहृदया न पश्यन्त्यन्तिकेऽन्तकम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ krameṇa jeṣyāmaḥ pṛthvīṃ sāgaramekhalām ityāśābaddhahṛdayā na paśyantyantike

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

translation:
(They plan in their minds):
“Having first conquered the group of six (Five cognitive senses+Minḍ) of our own self, we shall subjugate the counsellers, executive officers, relatives or confidants, citizens, conductors of elephants or army officers of other kings and then after removing all “thorns” (socially undesirable elements), we shall thus gradually bring under our control the earth engirdled by the seas.” These kings whose hearts are thus tied down to their hopes and desires, do not perceive the god of Death who is near (and ready to snatch them away).

devanagari: समुद्रावरणां जित्वा मां विशन्त्यब्धिमोजसा । कियदात्मजयस्यैतन्मुक्तिरात्मजये फलम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: samudrāvaraṇāṃ jitvā māṃ viśantyabdhimojasā kiyadātmajayasyaitanmuktirātmajaye phalam

[nominative single from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ viś class 6 verb]
[imperative active second single], [imperative passive first single]

translation: Having conquered me as far as the sea, some of them (being more ambitious) enter the sea (for naval victory and conquest of other island-continents). “Of what importance is this conquest against our self-control? Emancipation from Saṃsāra is the proper reward of self-control.”[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: Extremely dull-witted are they who desire to attain sovereignty by practising control over senses.
Anvitārthaprakāśikā: Sovereignty being transitory is an insignificant gain as a result of self-control; emancipation from the misery of Saṃsāra is the real recompense, for it is eternal.

devanagari: यां विसृज्यैव मनवः तत्सुताश्च कुरूद्वह । गता यथागतं युद्धे तां मां जेष्यन्त्यबुद्धयः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: yāṃ visṛjyaiva manavaḥ tatsutāśca kurūdvaha gatā yathāgataṃ yuddhe tāṃ māṃ jeṣyantyabuddhayaḥ

translation: O scion of the Kuru race! These foolish kings wish to conquer me through war, when great Manus and their sons and descendants had ultimately to abandon me and to depart as they came (leaving me here behind).

devanagari: मत्कृते पितृपुत्राणां भ्रातृणां चापि विग्रहः । जायते ह्यसतां राज्ये ममताबद्धचेतसाम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: matkṛte pitṛputrāṇāṃ bhrātṛṇāṃ cāpi vigrahaḥ jāyate hyasatāṃ rājye mamatābaddhacetasām

translation: Feuds and fights for me break out between fathers and sons, and between brothers and between those wicked Kings- whose minds are possessed with the notion of mine-ness with reference to their kingdoms.

devanagari: ममैवेयं मही कृत्स्ना न ते मूढेति वादिनः । स्पर्धमाना मिथो घ्नन्ति म्रियन्ते मत्कृते नृपाः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: mamaiveyaṃ mahī kṛtsnā na te mūḍheti vādinaḥ spardhamānā mitho ghnanti mriyante matkṛte nṛpāḥ

translation: “This entire earth belongs to me only and not yours, O fool. “Contending and competing thus, kings kill each other and die for my sake.”

devanagari: पृथुः पुरूरवा गाधिः नहुषो भरतोऽर्जुनः । मान्धाता सगरो रामः खट्वाङ्गो धुन्धुहा रघुः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthuḥ purūravā gādhiḥ nahuṣo bharato māndhātā sagaro rāmaḥ khaṭvāṅgo dhundhuhā raghuḥ
translation: Pṛthu, Purūravas, Gādhi, Nahuṣa, Bharata, Arjuna (of a thousand arms Sahasrārjuna), Māndhātā, Sagara, Rāma, Khaṭvāṅga, Dhundhumāra, Raghu, Tṛṇabindu, Yayāti, Śaryāti, Śantanu, Gaya, Bhagīratha, Kuvalayāśva, Kākutstha, Nala (the king of Niṣadha), Nṛga, Hiraṇya-Kaśipu, Vṛtra, Rāvaṇa, the scourge of the people (who made people to cry), Namuci, Śambara, Naraka, Hiraṇyākṣa and Tāraka, and many other Daityas and Kings who were the emperors of the world, all possessed of knowledge, valiant, vanquishers of all others but of themselves, remained unconquered.

devanagari: तृणबिन्दुर्ययातिश्च शर्यातिः शन्तनुर्गयः । भगीरथः कुवलयाश्वः ककुत्स्थो नैषधो नृगः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tṛṇabinduryayātiśca śaryātiḥ śantanurgayaḥ bhagīrathaḥ kuvalayāśvaḥ kakutstho naiṣadho nṛgaḥ
translation: Pṛthu, Purūravas, Gādhi, Nahuṣa, Bharata, Arjuna (of a thousand arms Sahasrārjuna), Māndhātā, Sagara, Rāma, Khaṭvāṅga, Dhundhumāra, Raghu, Tṛṇabindu, Yayāti, Śaryāti, Śantanu, Gaya, Bhagīratha, Kuvalayāśva, Kākutstha, Nala (the king of Niṣadha), Nṛga, Hiraṇya-Kaśipu, Vṛtra, Rāvaṇa, the scourge of the people (who made people to cry), Namuci, Śambara, Naraka, Hiraṇyākṣa and Tāraka, and many other Daityas and Kings who were the emperors of the world, all possessed of knowledge, valiant, vanquishers of all others but of themselves, remained unconquered.

devanagari: हिरण्यकशिपुर्वृत्रो रावणो लोकरावणः । नमुचिः शम्बरो भौमो हिरण्याक्षोऽथ तारकः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: hiraṇyakaśipurvṛtro rāvaṇo lokarāvaṇaḥ namuciḥ śambaro bhaumo hiraṇyākṣo
translation: Pṛthu, Purūravas, Gādhi, Nahuṣa, Bharata, Arjuna (of a thousand arms Sahasrārjuna), Māndhātā, Sagara, Rāma, Khaṭvāṅga, Dhundhumāra, Raghu, Tṛṇabindu, Yayāti, Śaryāti, Śantanu, Gaya, Bhagīratha, Kuvalayāśva, Kākutstha, Nala (the king of Niṣadha), Nṛga, Hiraṇya-Kaśipu, Vṛtra, Rāvaṇa, the scourge of the people (who made people to cry), Namuci, Śambara, Naraka, Hiraṇyākṣa and Tāraka, and many other Daityas and Kings who were the emperors of the world, all possessed of knowledge, valiant, vanquishers of all others but of themselves, remained unconquered.

devanagari: अन्ये च बहवो दैत्या राजानो ये महेश्वराः । सर्वे सर्वविदः शूराः सर्वे सर्वजितोऽजिताः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: anye ca bahavo daityā rājāno ye maheśvarāḥ sarve sarvavidaḥ śūrāḥ sarve sarvajito
translation: Pṛthu, Purūravas, Gādhi, Nahuṣa, Bharata, Arjuna (of a thousand arms Sahasrārjuna), Māndhātā, Sagara, Rāma, Khaṭvāṅga, Dhundhumāra, Raghu, Tṛṇabindu, Yayāti, Śaryāti, Śantanu, Gaya, Bhagīratha, Kuvalayāśva, Kākutstha, Nala (the king of Niṣadha), Nṛga, Hiraṇya-Kaśipu, Vṛtra, Rāvaṇa, the scourge of the people (who made people to cry), Namuci, Śambara, Naraka, Hiraṇyākṣa and Tāraka, and many other Daityas and Kings who were the emperors of the world, all possessed of knowledge, valiant, vanquishers of all others but of themselves, remained unconquered.

devanagari: ममतां मय्यवर्तन्त कृत्वोच्चैर्मर्त्यधर्मिणः । कथावशेषाः कालेन ह्यकृतार्थाः कृता विभो ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: mamatāṃ mayyavartanta kṛtvoccairmartyadharmiṇaḥ kathāvaśeṣāḥ kālena hyakṛtārthāḥ kṛtā vibho

translation: Though they were mortal by nature, they cherished the strongest attachment to me as if I belonged to them. But they were frustrated in accomplishing their desires, and were reduced as names in legends, by the inexorable Time or Death, O King.

devanagari: कथा इमास्ते कथिता महीयसां । विताय लोकेषु यशः परेयुषाम् । विज्ञानवैराग्यविवक्षया विभो । वचोविभूतीर्न तु पारमार्थ्यम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: kathā imāste kathitā mahīyasāṃ vitāya lokeṣu yaśaḥ pareyuṣām vijñānavairāgyavivakṣayā vibho vacovibhūtīrna tu pāramārthyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kath class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ kath class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ kath class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ kath class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ kath class 10 verb]

translation: O King Parīkṣit! It is with the intention of elucidating (the knowledge of) the unsubstantial nature of the world and to create (a sense of) renunciation about it, that these legends of those illustrious persons who after spreading their great renown in this world, had passed away, have been narrated to you. And all this (picturesque) narration is not mere display of words but bears vital relation to the highest truth![2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Although respectable annotators like Bhāvāratha Dīpikā Bhāgavata Candrikā interpret quite to the contrary, viz. ‘The non-relation of these legends to the highest or real purpose’, I feel that the explanation of Anvitārthaprakāśikā, suits the context better, despite the next verse which contrasts the difference between the glory of these kings and that of the incarnations of Viṣṇu and exhorts one to listen to the glory of Lord Kṛṣṇa constantly.

devanagari: यस्तु उत्तमश्लोकगुणानुवादः । संगीयतेऽभीक्ष्णममङ्गलघ्नः । तमेव नित्यं श्रृणुयादभीक्ष्णं । कृष्णेऽमलां भक्तिमभीप्समानः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: yastu uttamaślokaguṇānuvādaḥ saṃgīyate tameva nityaṃ śrṛṇuyādabhīkṣṇaṃ kṛṣṇe

translation:
But if a person is desirous of attaining pure (selfless) devotion unto Lord Kṛṣṇa he should incessantly listen every day to the exposition of the excellent attributes and great deeds of the Lord of hallowing renown (viz, Viṣṇu) which are always glorified in songs (by his votaries and pious persons) and which destroy all evils and inauspiciousness.
(Thinking that devotion unto the Lord cannot be engendered due to the evil influence of the Kali age, the King asks)

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । केनोपायेन भगवन् कलेर्दोषान् कलौ जनाः । विधमिष्यन्त्युपचितान् तन्मे ब्रूहि यथा मुने ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca kenopāyena bhagavan kalerdoṣān kalau janāḥ vidhamiṣyantyupacitān tanme brūhi yathā mune

translation:
King Parīkṣit enquired:
“Be pleased to explain to me (in details) the course resorting to which, people can shake off and destroy the accumulated evils of the Kali in the Kali age itself, O revered sage!

devanagari: युगानि युगधर्मांश्च मानं प्रलयकल्पयोः । कालस्येश्वररूपस्य गतिं विष्णोर्महात्मनः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: yugāni yugadharmāṃśca mānaṃ pralayakalpayoḥ kālasyeśvararūpasya gatiṃ viṣṇormahātmanaḥ

[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Kindly tell me (something) about the Yugas (the Ages or Aeons), the special course of duties prescribed for each Yuga, the duration of the life and the deluge ṭPralaya) of the universe, the course of the omnipotent Time-spirit which is the potency of the great-souled Lord Viṣṇu.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । कृते प्रवर्तते धर्मः चतुष्पात् तज्जनैर्धृतः । सत्यं दया तपो दानं इति पादा विभोर्नृप ॥ १८ ॥

[nominative dual from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka began to narrate:
In the Kṛta Age, Dharma (righteous course of conduct) as adhered to by the people of that age, proceeds on all its four legs. And Truth, compassion, penance, (selfcontrol and meditation) and affording protection to all (irrespective of friendly or inimical relations) are the four legs of that Perfect Dharma, O King.

devanagari: सन्तुष्टाः करुणा मैत्राः शान्ता दान्ताः तितिक्षवः । आत्मारामाः समदृशः प्रायशः श्रमणा जनाः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: santuṣṭāḥ karuṇā maitrāḥ śāntā dāntāḥ titikṣavaḥ ātmārāmāḥ samadṛśaḥ prāyaśaḥ śramaṇā janāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]

[nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: The people of that age were generally full of contentment and compassion, friendly to all beings, serene and peaceful, self-controlled, forebearing, finding spiritual bliss in their souls, looking upon all as equals (and practising no discrimination between their people and others) and engaging themselves in spiritual exercises such as meditation, etc.

devanagari: त्रेतायां धर्मपादानां तुर्यांशो हीयते शनैः । अधर्मपादैः अनृत हिंसासंतोषविग्रहैः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tretāyāṃ dharmapādānāṃ turyāṃśo hīyate śanaiḥ adharmapādaiḥ anṛta hiṃsāsaṃtoṣavigrahaiḥ
translation: In the Tretā Age, one-fourth part of each of the leg of Dharma gets gradually disabled by each of the four legs of Adharma (unrighteousness) viz., falsehood violence, discontent and discords respectively.

devanagari: तदा क्रियातपोनिष्ठा नातिहिंस्रा न लम्पटाः । त्रैवर्गिकास्त्रयीवृद्धा वर्णा ब्रह्मोत्तरा नृप ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā kriyātaponiṣṭhā nātihiṃsrā na lampaṭāḥ traivargikāstrayīvṛddhā varṇā brahmottarā nṛpa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]

translation: In that age, people were keen on performance of Vedic rites and were devoted to austere penance. (The five sins—pañca-śūnās—in the life of a house-holder are inevitable but people were not given to too much of violence. Nor were they lewd or addicted to the pleasure of senses. They used to exert for the first three purposes of human life, viz. Dharma (religious merit), Artha (wealth) and Kama (worldly pleasures, enjoyment). They were sincerely devoted to and advanced in Vedic studies, and Brāhmaṇas were regarded to be the highest Varṇa (class of society).

devanagari: तपःसत्यदयादानेषु अर्धं ह्रस्वति द्वापरे । हिंसातुष्ट्यनृतद्वेषैः धर्मस्याधर्मलक्षणैः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tapaḥsatyadayādāneṣu ardhaṃ hrasvati dvāpare hiṃsātuṣṭyanṛtadveṣaiḥ dharmasyādharmalakṣaṇaiḥ
translation: In the Dvāpara age, the four feet of the Dharma, viz. Penance, Truthfulness, Compassion and Protective and charitable attitude to all diminish by half with (the growing influence of) the four characteristics of unrighteousness (adharma) viz. violence, discontent or covetiousness, falsehood and hatred.

devanagari: यशस्विनो महाशीलाः स्वाध्यायाध्ययने रताः । आढ्याः कुटुम्बिनो हृष्टा वर्णाः क्षत्रद्विजोत्तराः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yaśasvino mahāśīlāḥ svādhyāyādhyayane ratāḥ āḍhyāḥ kuṭumbino hṛṣṭā varṇāḥ kṣatradvijottarāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ram class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ram class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ ram class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ram class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ram class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

[nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: In that age, people hankered after fame or glory, were prone to the performance of great sacrifices, indulging in the teaching and the study of the Vedas, endowed with great affluence, maintaining big families, joyous by nature. Brāhmaṇas and Kṣattriyas were then the most important classes of society.

devanagari: कलौ तु धर्महेतूनां तुर्यांशोऽधर्महेतुभिः । एधमानैः क्षीयमाणो ह्यन्ते सोऽपि विनङ्क्ष्यति ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: kalau tu dharmahetūnāṃ turyāṃśo edhamānaiḥ kṣīyamāṇo hyante so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṣī class 9 verb]

translation: In the Kali age, only one quarter of each of the four feet of Dharma remains. And that too goes on diminishing day by day by the powerfully increasing ‘feet’ of Adharma (unrighteousness) to such an extent that ultimately it becomes extinct.

devanagari: तस्मिन् लुब्धा दुराचारा निर्दयाः शुष्कवैरिणः । दुर्भगा भूरितर्षाश्च शूद्रदासोत्तराः प्रजाः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin lubdhā durācārā nirdayāḥ śuṣkavairiṇaḥ durbhagā bhūritarṣāśca śūdradāsottarāḥ prajāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ lubh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ lubh class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ lubh class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ lubh class 6 verb]

[nominative plural from √ lubh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ lubh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ lubh class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ lubh class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ lubh class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ lubh class 6 verb]

translation: In that (Kali) age, people will be greedy, take to wicked behaviour, will be merciless, indulge in hostilities without any cause, unfortunate, extremely covetous for wealth and women (or worldly desires) and higher social status will be attained by Śūdras, fishermen and such other (former backward) classes.

devanagari: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति दृश्यन्ते पुरुषे गुणाः । कालसञ्चोदितास्ते वै परिवर्तन्त आत्मनि ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: sattvaṃ rajastama iti dṛśyante puruṣe guṇāḥ kālasañcoditāste vai parivartanta ātmani

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: The attributes viz. Sattva, rajas and tamas become manifest in men. And being impelled by the force of the Timespirit, their influence varies (waxes or wanes).

devanagari: प्रभवन्ति यदा सत्त्वे मनोबुद्धीन्द्रियाणि च । तदा कृतयुगं विद्यात् ज्ञाने तपसि यद्रुचिः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: prabhavanti yadā sattve manobuddhīndriyāṇi ca tadā kṛtayugaṃ vidyāt jñāne tapasi yadruciḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: When the mind, intellect and sense-organs are dominated by Sattva and when there is genuine liking for (spiritual) wisdom and knowledge and performance of asceticism, one should know that it is the Kṛta age.

devanagari: यदा धर्मार्थ कामेषु भक्तिर्यशसि देहिनाम् । तदा त्रेता रजोवृत्तिः इति जानीहि बुद्धिमन् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā dharmārtha kāmeṣu bhaktiryaśasi dehinām tadā tretā rajovṛttiḥ iti jānīhi buddhiman
translation: O intelligent king! When embodied beings (people) show devotion and liking for the first three purposes of human life, viz, dharma, artha and Kāma and the attribute called rajas controls the attitude of people, note that that is the Tretā Age.

devanagari: यदा लोभस्तु असन्तोषो मानो दम्भोऽथ मत्सरः । कर्मणां चापि काम्यानां द्वापरं तद् रजस्तमः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā lobhastu asantoṣo māno dambho karmaṇāṃ cāpi kāmyānāṃ dvāparaṃ tad rajastamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: When the time (i.e. the attitude of people) is controlled by a mixture of rajas and tamas, and when greed, discontent, pride, hypocrisy, jealousy predominate and men like to perform religious acts for achieving desirable ends, it is the Dvāpara Age.

devanagari: यदा मायानृतं तन्द्रा निद्रा हिंसा विषादनम् । शोकमोहौ भयं दैन्यं स कलिस्तामसः स्मृतः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yadā māyānṛtaṃ tandrā nidrā hiṃsā viṣādanam śokamohau bhayaṃ dainyaṃ sa kalistāmasaḥ smṛtaḥ
translation: When deceit, falsehood, lethargy, sleepiness, violence, despondency, grief, delusion, fear, poverty-stricken-ness prevail, that is the Kali Age which is characterised by tamas.

devanagari: यस्मात् क्षुद्रदृशो मर्त्याः क्षुद्रभाग्या महाशनाः । कामिनो वित्तहीनाश्च स्वैरिण्यश्च स्त्रियोऽसतीः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: yasmāt kṣudradṛśo martyāḥ kṣudrabhāgyā mahāśanāḥ kāmino vittahīnāśca svairiṇyaśca striyo
translation: As a result of Kali’s influence, mortal beings become dull-witted, unlucky, voracious, destitute of wealth yet voluptuous, and women, wanton and unchaste.

devanagari: दस्यूत्कृष्टा जनपदा वेदाः पाषण्डदूषिताः । राजानश्च प्रजाभक्षाः शिश्नोदरपरा द्विजाः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: dasyūtkṛṣṭā janapadā vedāḥ pāṣaṇḍadūṣitāḥ rājānaśca prajābhakṣāḥ śiśnodaraparā dvijāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ duṣ ], [vocative plural from √ duṣ ]
[nominative plural from √ duṣ ], [vocative plural from √ duṣ ], [accusative plural from √ duṣ ]

translation: Countries will be ravaged by robbers and miscreants; the Vedas will be condemned by heretics; kings will exploit the subjects (lit. Rulers will be men-eaters), and twice-borns like Brāhmaṇas will be given to the gratification of their sexual ḍesires and other appetites (lit. filling the belly).

devanagari: अव्रता वटवोऽशौचा भिक्षवश्च कुटुम्बिनः । तपस्विनो ग्रामवासा न्यासिनोऽत्यर्थलोलुपाः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: avratā vaṭavo tapasvino grāmavāsā nyāsino
translation: Celibates (belonging to the Brahmacarya stage of life) will cease to observe their prescribed vows of study, purity and celibacy; householders (in stead of helping others by alms-giving) will take to begging; hermits (of the Vānaprastha stage will leave forests where they retire for penance), will resort to villages (and towns) and Sannyāsins will be extremely greedy of money.

devanagari: ह्रस्वकाया महाहारा भूर्यपत्या गतह्रियः । शश्वत्कटुकभाषिण्यः चौर्यमायोरुसाहसाः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: hrasvakāyā mahāhārā bhūryapatyā gatahriyaḥ śaśvatkaṭukabhāṣiṇyaḥ cauryamāyorusāhasāḥ
translation: Women will be short-statured but voracious, noted for fecundity and shameless. They will be harsh-speakers, given to theft, fraud and dare-devilry.

devanagari: पणयिष्यन्ति वै क्षुद्राः किराटाः कूटकारिणः । अनापद्यपि मंस्यन्ते वार्तां साधु जुगुप्सिताम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: paṇayiṣyanti vai kṣudrāḥ kirāṭāḥ kūṭakāriṇaḥ anāpadyapi maṃsyante vārtāṃ sādhu jugupsitām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ paṇ ], [vocative plural from √ paṇ ], [accusative plural from √ paṇ ]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Petty-minded people (with no moral values) conduct business transactions and merchants will be fraudulent. Even when there is no emergency or imminent danger people will follow trades or pursuits censured by the righteous.

devanagari: पतिं त्यक्ष्यन्ति निर्द्रव्यं भृत्याप्यखिलोत्तमम् । भृत्यं विपन्नं पतयः कौलं गाश्चापयस्विनीः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: patiṃ tyakṣyanti nirdravyaṃ bhṛtyāpyakhilottamam bhṛtyaṃ vipannaṃ patayaḥ kaulaṃ gāścāpayasvinīḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

translation: Servants will resign their services of the masters who are excellent in all respects but have become penniless. Masters will dismiss even their hereditary servants when they are in distress (through physical disability, etc.) and people will not maintain cows when they cease to yield milk.

devanagari: पितृभ्रातृसुहृत्ज्ञातीन् हित्वा सौरतसौहृदाः । ननान्दृश्यालसंवादा दीनाः स्त्रैणाः कलौ नराः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: pitṛbhrātṛsuhṛtjñātīn hitvā sauratasauhṛdāḥ nanāndṛśyālasaṃvādā dīnāḥ straiṇāḥ kalau narāḥ
translation: In the Kali age, men will abandon their parents, brothers, friends and relatives and establish their friendliness on sexual basis. Their affection being centred on their relation with women, they will seek consultations from their wives’ relatives (such as sisters and brothers-in-laws) and will be miserable.

devanagari: शूद्राः प्रतिग्रहीष्यन्ति तपोवेषोपजीविनः । धर्मं वक्ष्यन्त्यधर्मज्ञा अधिरुह्योत्तमासनम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: śūdrāḥ pratigrahīṣyanti tapoveṣopajīvinaḥ dharmaṃ vakṣyantyadharmajñā adhiruhyottamāsanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ iṣ class 4 verb]

[nominative dual from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ vac class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative dual from √ vac class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ vac class 3 verb], [vocative dual from √ vac class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative dual from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

[nominative dual from √ vah class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ vah class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vah class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vah class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vah class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vah class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ vah class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vah class 1 verb]

translation: Earning their livelihood under the guise (and makeup) of recluses and sages, Śūdras will accept charities. People ignorant of religion, will occupy high seats (and pulpits) and will (pose to) preach religion.

devanagari: नित्यमुद्विग्नमनसो दुर्भिक्षकरकर्शिताः । निरन्ने भूतले राजन् अनावृष्टिभयातुराः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: nityamudvignamanaso durbhikṣakarakarśitāḥ niranne bhūtale rājan anāvṛṣṭibhayāturāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛś ], [vocative plural from √ kṛś ]
[nominative plural from √ kṛś ], [vocative plural from √ kṛś ], [accusative plural from √ kṛś ]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: People will have their minds weighed down with constant anxiety and fear, due to devastating famines and heavy taxation. The land being devoid of food-crops, people will be always afflicted with the impending fear of droughts.

devanagari: वासोऽन्नपानशयनव्यवायस्नानभूषणैः । हीनाः पिशाचसन्दर्शा भविष्यन्ति कलौ प्रजाः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: vāso hīnāḥ piśācasandarśā bhaviṣyanti kalau prajāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hā class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ hā class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ hā class 3 verb]

[nominative plural from √ hā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hā class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hā class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ hā class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ hā class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ hā class 3 verb]

translation: Being destitute of clothing, food, drink, bed, sexual satisfaction, bath and adornments, people in the Kali age, will appear like ghosts.

devanagari: कलौ काकिणिकेऽप्यर्थे विगृह्य त्यक्तसौहृदाः । त्यक्ष्यन्ति च प्रियान्प्राणान् हनिष्यन्ति स्वकानपि ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: kalau kākiṇike tyakṣyanti ca priyānprāṇān haniṣyanti svakānapi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

[nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: In the Kali age, even for a paltry amount of a Kākiṇikā (20 cowries), people will fight, throwing to winds all friendliness and good will. They will even kill their own relatives or give up their dear lives (commit suicide for such a petty amount).

devanagari: न रक्षिष्यन्ति मनुजाः स्थविरौ पितरौ अपि । पुत्रान् सर्वार्थकुशलान् क्षुद्राः शिश्नोदरम्भराः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: na rakṣiṣyanti manujāḥ sthavirau pitarau api putrān sarvārthakuśalān kṣudrāḥ śiśnodarambharāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ rakṣ class 1 verb]

translation: People will not protect even their aged parents. Low- minded persons bent on satisfying their sex and filling the belly will not care for their sons even though they may be clever and skilful in all respects.

devanagari: कलौ न राजन् जगतां परं गुरुं । त्रिलोकनाथानतपादपङ्कजम् । प्रायेण मर्त्या भगवन्तमच्युतं । यक्ष्यन्ति पाषण्डविभिन्नचेतसः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: kalau na rājan jagatāṃ paraṃ guruṃ trilokanāthānatapādapaṅkajam prāyeṇa martyā bhagavantamacyutaṃ yakṣyanti pāṣaṇḍavibhinnacetasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: O King! During the Kali age, the minds of the human beings being perverted by heretic doctrines[3] will not generally worship Lord Acyuta, the supreme Father and Preceptor of the worlds, the lotus-feet of whom are bowed by the lords of the three worlds.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Padaratnāvalī takes this opportunity to hit at the advaitins and explains Pākhaṇḍa-Vibhinna-Cetasaḥ as those whose minds are deluded by the heretical doctrine known as advaita-śāstra.

devanagari: यन्नामधेयं म्रियमाण आतुरः । पतन् स्खलन् वा विवशो गृणन् पुमान् । विमुक्तकर्मार्गल उत्तमां गतिं । प्राप्नोति यक्ष्यन्ति न तं कलौ जनाः ॥ ४...
english_devnagari: yannāmadheyaṃ mriyamāṇa āturaḥ patan skhalan vā vivaśo gṛṇan pumān vimuktakarmārgala uttamāṃ gatiṃ prāpnoti yakṣyanti na taṃ kalau janāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

[nominative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [locative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

[nominative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ skhal class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ skhal class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: During the Kali age, people will not worship the Lord by uttering unawares whose name even unconsciously, a dying man or a person afflicted (with care or disease), falling or trembling, becomes absolved and free from all the impediments of Karmas and attains to the highest goal (viz. mokṣa- emancipation from saṃsāra).

devanagari: पुंसां कलिकृतान् दोषान् द्रव्यदेशात्मसंभवान् । सर्वान् हरति चित्तस्थो भगवान् पुरुषोत्तमः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: puṃsāṃ kalikṛtān doṣān dravyadeśātmasaṃbhavān sarvān harati cittastho bhagavān puruṣottamaḥ
translation: The Supreme Person, the glorious Lord Viṣṇu abides in the hearts of all and dispels all the evils of men arising out of the influence of Kali from substances, place and mind.

devanagari: श्रुतः सङ्कीर्तितो ध्यातः पूजितश्चादृतोऽपि वा । नृणां धुनोति भगवान् हृत्स्थो जन्मायुताशुभम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutaḥ saṅkīrtito dhyātaḥ pūjitaścādṛto nṛṇāṃ dhunoti bhagavān hṛtstho janmāyutāśubham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ pūj class 10 verb]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: If the Lord enthroned in the heart (of all) is listened to or glorified (by chanting his name or singing his glory) or meditated upon, worshipped or even paid respect to (by some other way), he shakes off (and thus absolves man of) his sins committed and accumulated in thousands of births.

devanagari: यथा हेम्नि स्थितो वह्निः दुर्वर्णं हन्ति धातुजम् । एवं आत्मगतो विष्णुः योगिनां अशुभाशयम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā hemni sthito vahniḥ durvarṇaṃ hanti dhātujam evaṃ ātmagato viṣṇuḥ yogināṃ aśubhāśayam
translation: Even as the fire applied to gold burns off and thus destroys all the impurities or metallic alloys mixed with gold. Lord Viṣṇu who is abiding in the heart (or Soul) destroys all evil thoughts (or sins) in the hearts of Yogins.

devanagari: विद्यातपःप्राणनिरोधमैत्री । तीर्थाभिषेकव्रतदानजप्यैः । नात्यन्तशुद्धिं लभतेऽन्तरात्मा । यथा हृदिस्थे भगवत्यनन्ते ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: vidyātapaḥprāṇanirodhamaitrī tīrthābhiṣekavratadānajapyaiḥ nātyantaśuddhiṃ labhate yathā hṛdisthe bhagavatyanante

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: The inner heart of a man does not attain that much absolute purity by means of (Vedic or Śāstric) learning, penance, breath-control (prāṇāyāma), sense of friendliness to all beings, ablutions in sacred waters, observance of vows, giving of (munificent) charitable gifts and muttering silently (His prayer), as it does by (meditating upon) the infinite Lord who is enthroned in the heart.

devanagari: तस्मात् सर्वात्मना राजन् हृदिस्थं कुरु केशवम् । म्रियमाणो ह्यवहितः ततो यासि परां गतिम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt sarvātmanā rājan hṛdisthaṃ kuru keśavam mriyamāṇo hyavahitaḥ tato yāsi parāṃ gatim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

translation: Hence, O King Parīkṣit, with all your power of concentration, enthrone Lord Keśava in your heart. Meditating upon him with full concentration at the time of death, you will attain the highest goal (viz. Mokṣa).

devanagari: म्रियमाणैरभिध्येयो भगवान् परमेश्वरः । आत्मभावं नयत्यङ्ग सर्वात्मा सर्वसंश्रयः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: mriyamāṇairabhidhyeyo bhagavān parameśvaraḥ ātmabhāvaṃ nayatyaṅga sarvātmā sarvasaṃśrayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [instrumental plural from √ mṛ class 6 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [instrumental plural from √ mṛ class 6 verb]
[nominative dual from √ nay class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ nay class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ nay class 1 verb], [locative single from √ nay class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ nī class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ nī class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ nī class 1 verb], [locative single from √ nī class 1 verb]

translation: This glorious Lord who is the supreme Ruler of the universe should be meditated upon with full concentration, by persons who are about to die. Dear Parīkṣit! He is the Soul and supporter of all. He leads them to the state of being one with him.

devanagari: कलेर्दोषनिधे राजन् अस्ति ह्येको महान् गुणः । कीर्तनात् एव कृष्णस्य मुक्तसङ्गः परं व्रजेत् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: kalerdoṣanidhe rājan asti hyeko mahān guṇaḥ kīrtanāt eva kṛṣṇasya muktasaṅgaḥ paraṃ vrajet

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Kali is certainly the Store-house of all evils. But O king, there is one very great virtue and a good point in that age, inasmuch as by singing the name and the glory of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, that person is freed from all attachments and attains to the highest region (Vaikuṇṭha ).

devanagari: कृते यद् ध्यायतो विष्णुं त्रेतायां यजतो मखैः । द्वापरे परिचर्यायां कलौ तद् हरिकीर्तनात् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛte yad dhyāyato viṣṇuṃ tretāyāṃ yajato makhaiḥ dvāpare paricaryāyāṃ kalau tad harikīrtanāt

[accusative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ yaj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ yaj class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ yaj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation:
What one achieves by meditating upon Lord Viṣṇu in the Kṛta age, by performance of sacrifices in the Tretā age, by worship and service of the Lord in the Dvāpara Age, a person attains that in the Kali age by chanting the name of Hari.
